title: Tumbling, sliding, and session windows
description: How disjoint tumbling, overlapping sliding, and gap-driven session windows differ in coverage and event membership.
body: Use when picking how events group over time. Tumbling windows are fixed, non-overlapping intervals so each event belongs to exactly one; sliding windows advance by a step smaller than their length so events fall into several overlapping windows; session windows group bursts separated by an inactivity gap and have variable length. Do not use sliding when you mean disjoint periods — it multiplies each event across windows. Actions: choose by whether periods overlap and whether boundaries are fixed or activity-driven. Validation signals include each event's window membership matching the chosen type and aggregates matching a manual check. Risk boundary: a wrong type distorts every aggregate; if counts disagree with a reference, revisit the window definition first.
